objective: Explore an end-to-end topology rewrite future for the current ideation problem. Apply that branch specifically to 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer'.
lane_strategy: Break one core assumption and test a high-novelty replacement for the current task.

model_audit.routing_basis: AAS4 ordinary FULL_PIPELINE / IDEATION default future-branch routing target.

notes: Replaced the bootstrap placeholder with a real Gamma lane systems_thinker child result.
Only the assigned child artifact was updated; no shared-state surfaces were edited.

evidence.note: The branch objective is an end-to-end topology rewrite, so the fit test is whether the idea should remain an independent layer or be re-expressed as a full-stack substrate rewrite under existing Alternative C authority.

Affected module: C45 route compiler and execution-mode lock. C45 already owns route admission, native-versus-leased substrate choice, provider scrubbing, provenance sealing, and fallback, so the rewritten topology must place the cognition substrate inside C45 rather than beside it.

Affected module: C42 priming and runtime-handoff broker. C42 already separates warm state, continuation, and execution priming from actual runtime authorization, so heavy-model execution must stay lease-visible and subordinate to C23 instead of becoming ambient microservice access.

Affected module: C47 model supply and promotion lane. C47 already owns open-source model intake, adaptation, quarantine, and membrane-scoped promotion, so the topology rewrite should consume promoted model families from Forge instead of inventing duplicate model-lifecycle authority.

Affected module: C36 membrane boundary. EMA-I rejects generic gateway thinking, exposes enterprise requests through C45-compatible bounded execution descriptors, and forbids direct Sanctum reachability, so the rewritten topology cannot introduce a new external inference endpoint plane.

Affected module: C39 delegated sampling semantics. C39 already defines sampling_request and sampling_result with explicit model provenance and default deny for tool use, so the topology rewrite should reuse this message family rather than add a parallel inference protocol.

Affected module: C18 compute economics. C18 already prices sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge adaptation as first-order budget surfaces, which means the rewritten topology needs explicit placement and capacity governance instead of pretending a new layer is economically neutral.

Affected module: C22 rollout and service decomposition. C22 already assumes bounded leased-provider wrappers, local-native fallback, and spec-boundary implementation, so the end-to-end rewrite belongs in execution placement and rollout sequencing rather than as a standalone microservice architecture.

Affected module: T-300 authority boundary. T-300 fixes C39, C42, C45, C47, and related Alternative C surfaces as forward authority, so any rewrite that revives a separately legible communication-era inference layer would be noncompliant.

recommended_next_action: Advance only if the idea is reframed from 'independent model inference microservice layer' into an internal sovereign cognition substrate split across existing modules: C45 for route admission and placement, C42 for priming and runtime handoff, C47 for native model promotion, C39 for delegated sampling semantics, C36 for membrane exposure, C18 for capacity economics, C22 for rollout sequencing, all under the T-300 Alternative C boundary.
